questions.q: In which Indian city is Humayun's Tomb located?
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb stands in Delhi, in the Nizamuddin area near the Yamuna river.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is the mausoleum of a ruler of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Humayun was the second Mughal emperor, and the tomb was built for him.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is often called the subcontinent's first great example of what type of tomb?
questions.explanation: It was the first major garden-tomb in the Indian subcontinent, set within a formal char-bagh garden.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is widely regarded as a forerunner of which famous later monument?
questions.explanation: Its design and garden setting strongly influenced the later Taj Mahal at Agra.

questions.q: In which century was Humayun's Tomb built?
questions.explanation: The tomb was constructed in the mid-16th century, in the years after Humayun's death in 1556.

questions.q: What international heritage recognition has Humayun's Tomb received?
options: New Seven Wonders of the World
questions.explanation: Humayun's Tomb was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is set in a formal garden divided into quarters. What is this garden layout called?
questions.explanation: The tomb sits within a char-bagh, a Persian four-part garden divided by water channels and walkways.

questions.q: Which prominent feature crowns the top of Humayun's Tomb?
questions.explanation: The structure is topped by a large double dome clad in white marble.

questions.q: What is the dominant building stone that gives Humayun's Tomb its reddish color?
questions.explanation: The tomb is built mainly of red sandstone, accented with white and black marble.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb was built primarily as what kind of structure?
questions.explanation: It is a mausoleum, built to house the grave of Emperor Humayun.

questions.q: Who is most commonly credited with commissioning the construction of Humayun's Tomb?
options: His widow, Empress Bega Begum (Haji Begum)
questions.explanation: The tomb is traditionally said to have been commissioned by Humayun's widow, Bega Begum, also known as Haji Begum.

questions.q: Which architect is credited with designing Humayun's Tomb?
questions.explanation: The Persian architect Mirak Mirza Ghiyas (Mirak Mirza Ghiyath) is credited with the tomb's design.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is built in a style that blends Indian craft with the architecture of which other region?
questions.explanation: The tomb fuses Persian (Timurid) architectural ideas with Indian materials and craftsmanship.

questions.q: In which year did Emperor Humayun die, prompting the eventual construction of his tomb?
questions.explanation: Humayun died in 1556 after falling down the steps of his library in Delhi.

questions.q: Construction of Humayun's Tomb is generally dated to roughly which period?
questions.explanation: The tomb was built over several years in the 1560s and completed around 1570 during Akbar's reign.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb is described as having a 'double dome.' What does this mean?
options: An outer shell and an inner shell forming the dome
A dome made of two colors only
questions.explanation: A double dome has a high outer shell and a lower inner shell, a Persian technique used to balance exterior height with interior proportion.

questions.q: On which side of the Yamuna river area of Delhi does Humayun's Tomb stand, near a famous Sufi shrine?
options: Near the shrine of Nizamuddin Auliya
questions.explanation: The tomb lies close to the dargah of the Sufi saint Nizamuddin Auliya in Delhi.

questions.q: Because so many Mughal family members are buried there, Humayun's Tomb is sometimes nicknamed what?
questions.explanation: Many Mughals were interred in its chambers, earning it the nickname the 'dormitory of the Mughals.'

questions.q: The tomb building is raised on a large platform. What is this kind of elevated podium typically called?
questions.explanation: The mausoleum sits atop a high arcaded plinth, a podium that lifts and frames the tomb chamber.

questions.q: Which architectural feature, common in Mughal buildings, decorates the roofline of Humayun's Tomb as small domed kiosks?
questions.explanation: Small domed pavilions called chhatris surround the central white dome on the roof.

questions.q: A major conservation and garden restoration of the Humayun's Tomb complex in the 2000s was led with support from which philanthropic trust?
options: The Aga Khan Trust for Culture
questions.explanation: The Aga Khan Trust for Culture led a landmark restoration of the gardens and tomb in partnership with Indian authorities.

questions.q: The ground plan of the tomb's main chamber is based on which symmetrical arrangement?
options: A radially symmetrical plan with chambers around a central hall
questions.explanation: The tomb has a symmetrical plan with octagonal and connected chambers arranged around the central domed hall.

questions.q: During which Mughal emperor's reign was Humayun's Tomb completed?
questions.explanation: Though begun after Humayun's death, the tomb was completed during the reign of his son Akbar.

questions.q: Which Mughal ruler took refuge at Humayun's Tomb and was captured by the British there during the 1857 uprising?
questions.explanation: The last Mughal emperor, Bahadur Shah Zafar, took refuge at and was captured at Humayun's Tomb in 1857.

questions.q: The architect of Humayun's Tomb was brought to India from which region, reflecting the tomb's Persian character?
questions.explanation: Mirak Mirza Ghiyas was a Persian architect from the Herat region, bringing Timurid-Persian design to the project.

questions.q: Within the char-bagh, the four quarters are separated by water channels meeting at the center. What broader Islamic concept does this layout evoke?
questions.explanation: The four-part garden with flowing water symbolizes the Quranic paradise garden, a recurring Mughal theme.

questions.q: Which earlier structure within the wider Humayun's Tomb complex predates the main tomb and houses the grave of a noble of the Lodi-Sur period?
questions.explanation: The octagonal tomb of Isa Khan Niyazi, built in the 1540s, lies within the complex and predates Humayun's Tomb.

questions.q: The high outer dome of Humayun's Tomb is finished in white marble, contrasting with which decorative detailing in the sandstone facades?
options: Inlaid white and black marble patterns
questions.explanation: The red sandstone exterior is patterned with inlaid white and black marble, a hallmark of early Mughal stonework.

questions.q: Humayun's Tomb introduced to India the monumental arched recess centered in each facade. What is this Persian feature called?
questions.explanation: Each facade features a tall central arched portal, an iwan within a pishtaq frame, a key Persian element later seen in the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: The Humayun's Tomb complex was among the early monuments protected and later jointly conserved with which Indian government body responsible for heritage sites?
questions.explanation: The Archaeological Survey of India is the custodian of the monument and partnered in its conservation.
